properties.remark: Visibility less than 1/4 mile with blowing snow. Based on a sky camera and known distance to the Fritch/Borger highway.

Visibility dropped to 1/4 mile from 1535CST and lasted to about 1600CST due to blowing snow. Winds had picked up to 25 mph gusting to 35 mph. Temperatures also dropped from 20 degrees F to 14 degrees F with the squall passage.
Multiple vehicle pile-up on I-27 south of Canyon likely due to damaging wind and reduced visibility due to blowing dust. Injuries/fatalities are unknown at this time.

The tornado started near County Road 30 on the very eastern edge of Hemphill County and quickly intensified as it crossed onto property where it produced damage to several buildings and a home. The home sustained some roofing damage and the back corner of the house had a partial uplift of the roof deck. Additional storage buildings were damaged including shed doors blown off and roofs and siding damaged. Several trees were uprooted in the yard. A cattle wind break was partially torn from the ground. The tornado then crossed into Ellis County Oklahoma (NWS Norman forecast area), where it snapped a few tree limbs on E690. The tornado then crossed an open field and the last noted damage was along NS1690 where some large branches were snapped. Based on storm spotter footage and survey, it appears the tornado lifted shortly thereafter. The tornado was rated an EF1 based on the house damage and the tree damage.

Several visuals from storm spotters indicated that funnel clouds and perhaps brief touchdowns occurred before this tornado from the same supercell as it crossed western Deaf Smith County. However, this tornado did overturn a couple of pivots south of County Road 27 and east of Highway 214. This appears to have been the only damage noted from this tornado so the exact length of the track is unknown due to the lack of damage indicators. Max estimated winds were 85mph and estimated width was 50 yards. Estimated length was around 0.83 miles. Tornado was rated EF0.

Hail winds and hail occurred over large area just west of Dalhart to the state line generally extending from around Highway 87 to Highway 54. This impacted many farms and there was damage to crops reported throughout the area including some fields that were completely flattened and were a total loss.

Severe wind gusts occurred at the Dalhart 12SW mesonet site from a likely anvil gust from a nearby thunderstorm. The mesonet site initially gusted to 61mph at 0749pm, had its peak gust of 86mph at 750pm, and continued to gust to 79mph and 74mph at 751pm and 752pm respectively. Gusts diminished to subsevere after this.

EM reported windows knocked out of two cars in Felt. Supports downburst with winds likely over 80 mph.

A downburst event occurred near a feed lot 3 miles north of Felt and affected some parts surrounding. Roofs at the lot and at surrounding houses were torn but not completely ripped off. 6 power poles were knocked down. Some were completely snapped. Small trees were snapped in two. Large trees had branches snapped off. A few irrigation pivots were knocked over and broken. Finally, a stop sign was reported to have been uprooted and tossed 300 ft NNE from its original position. Photos were submitted by the Cimarron county Emergency Manager.
Fire engine damaged in route to wildfires caused by lightning. Hail sizes were mostly dime to quarters, but a few slightly larger than golf ball.
